channel.title: 2026-05-13 AI × Science 文献日报 RSS
channel.description: 2026-05-13 当天筛选出的 AI × 物理 / 化学 / 材料交叉文献 RSS。

item.title: Fast and Accurate Prediction of Lattice Thermal Conductivity via Machine Learning Surrogates
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zeyu Wang, Shuya Yamazaki, Martin Hoffmann Petersen, Masato Ohnishi, Tomiya Yamamoto, Wei Nong, Jianghai Wang, Ruiming Zhu, Masatoshi Hanai, Michimasa Morita, Toyotaro Suzumura, Zekun Ren, Junichiro Shiomi, Kedar Hippalgaonkar
arXiv:2605.11610v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The appearance of generative models has opened vast chemical spaces in the design of functional materials. Although machine learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs) have substantially accelerated phonon calculations, high-fidelity prediction of lattice thermal conductivity kappalat still requires accurate treatment of anharmonic interactions, which remains a key challenge for existing potentials across novel chemical spaces. To address this challenge, we present a comprehensive benchmark of 15 surrogate models for predicting kappalat using the Phonix database, which contains 6,966 entries with anharmonic phonon properties derived from first-principles calculations. Firstly, We categorize these surrogate models into three distinct groups: Phys…

item.title: Discovery of High-Voltage Magnesium-Ion Cathodes using Machine Learning and First-Principles Calculations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jhon Rogelnor A. Florida, Edward Aris D. Fajardo
arXiv:2605.12067v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Developing high-performance cathode materials for magnesium-ion batteries (MIBs) remains challenging because Mg²⁺ ions move slowly, and conventional materials exhibit low voltage outputs. In this study, machine learning and first-principles calculations were combined to investigate topological quantum materials (TQMs) as a new class of cathode candidates. A modified crystal graph convolutional neural network (mCGCNN) was used to screen 917 Mg-containing TQMs, identifying a small subset of materials with predicted voltages above 3 V and high volumetric capacities. Among these, Mg₂VO₄ and Mg₆MnO₈ were selected for detailed density functional theory (DFT) analysis. Formation energy and convex-hull calculations indicate that MgₓVO₄ exhibits a fu…

item.title: Automated multiphase identification and refinement in powder diffraction using mismatch-tolerant machine learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Lalit Yadav, Yongqiang Cheng, Mathieu Doucet
arXiv:2605.12478v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Powder diffraction is a primary structural characterization tool in materials science, yet automated phase identification remains a major bottleneck for autonomous discovery. Existing workflows rely heavily on search--match heuristics and manual Rietveld refinement, and broadly usable end-to-end automation is especially limited for neutron powder diffraction, where comparable tools are largely absent. Here we introduce RADAR-PD, a modality-aware machine learning framework for phase identification and quantification across both X-ray and neutron powder diffraction. RADAR-PD couples a mismatch-tolerant neural network operating on coarse momentum-transfer fingerprints with automated lattice nudging and physics-constrained Rietveld verification,…

item.title: Probing Non-Equilibrium Grain Boundary Dynamics with XPCS and Domain-Adaptive Machine Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Mouyang Cheng, Bowen Yu, Chu-Liang Fu, Nina Andrejevic, Matthias T. Agne, Riley Hanus, Qiwei Wan, Nathan C. Drucker, Thanh Nguyen, Andrei Fluerasu, Lutz Wiegart, Xiaoqian M Chen, Daniel Pajerowski, Yongqiang Cheng, Joshua J Turner, G. Jeffrey Snyder, Mingda Li
arXiv:2605.12194v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Grain-boundary (GB) dynamics control the stability, mechanical, and functional response of nanocrystalline materials, but direct experimental access to their slow non-equilibrium motion has been limited. Here we establish X-ray photon correlation spectroscopy (XPCS), combined with domain-adaptive machine learning, as a quantitative probe of GB dynamics. Temperature- and grain-size-dependent two-time XPCS measurements in nanocrystalline silicon reveal pronounced departures from time-translation invariance, showing that GB relaxation can remain far from equilibrium over experimental timescales. However, direct extraction of quantitative physical information from these high-dimensional, noisy fluctuation maps faces a significant challenge. To o…

item.title: Controlled Steering-Based State Preparation for Adversarial-Robust Quantum Machine Learning
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sahan Sanjaya, Hari Krishna Parvatham, Emma Andrews, Prabhat Mishra
arXiv:2605.10954v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Quantum machine learning (QML) provides a promising framework for leveraging quantum-mechanical effects in learning tasks. However, its vulnerability to adversarial perturbations remains a major challenge for practical deployment. In QML systems, small perturbations applied to classical inputs can propagate through the quantum encoding stage and distort the resulting quantum state, thereby degrading model performance. In this work, we propose a defense mechanism that replaces the conventional quantum encoding stage of a QML model with passive steering-based controlled state preparation, which guides the encoded state toward a controlled intermediate state. By tuning the steering strength and the number of steering iterations, the proposed…

item.title: Interpretable Machine Learning for Spatial Science: A Lie-Algebraic Kernel for Rotationally Anisotropic Gaussian Processes
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Kane Warrior, Dalia Chakrabarty
arXiv:2605.11179v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many three-dimensional spatial fields are anisotropic, with directions of rapid and slow variation that need not align with the coordinate axes. Standard Gaussian process kernels with Automatic Relevance Determination (ARD) capture only axis-aligned anisotropy, while generic full symmetric positive definite (SPD) metrics can represent rotated anisotropy but do not parameterise principal length-scales and directions directly. We introduce an interpretable rotationally anisotropic GP kernel that parameterises a three-dimensional SPD covariance metric using three principal length-scales and an explicit SO(3) rotation. The rotation is represented by an axis-angle vector and mapped to SO(3) via the Lie-algebra exponential map, giving unconstraine…

item.title: Physics-Informed Graph Neural Networks for Frequency-Aware Optical Aberration Correction
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yong En Kok, Bowen Deng, Alexander Bentley, Andrew J. Parkes, Michael G. Somekh, Amanda J. Wright, Michael P. Pound
arXiv:2512.05683v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Optical aberrations significantly degrade image quality in microscopy, particularly when imaging deeper into samples. These aberrations arise from distortions in the optical wavefront and can be mathematically represented using Zernike polynomials. Existing methods often address only mild aberrations on limited sample types and modalities, typically treating the problem as a black-box mapping without leveraging the underlying optical physics of wavefront distortions. We propose ZRNet, a physics-informed framework that jointly performs Zernike coefficient prediction and optical image Restoration. We contribute a Zernike Graph module that explicitly models physical relationships between Zernike polynomials based on their azimuthal de…

item.title: Coexistence and tunability of orbital and spin Hall effects in RuO₂
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Lishu Zhang, Mahmoud Zeer, Dongwook Go, Theodoros Adamantopoulos, Peter Schmitz, Stefan Blügel, Chengwang Niu, Yuriy Mokrousov, Shishen Yan, Hyunsoo Yang, Lei Shen
arXiv:2507.21480v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Altermagnetic materials, especially RuO₂, have recently attracted considerable attention for their unique magnetic properties and energy-efficient spintronic applications. However, recent experimental studies have reported highly conflicting signatures regarding altermagnetic spin splitting and charge--spin interconversion (CSI) in RuO₂. While some experiments link efficient CSI to non-relativistic altermagnetic spin-splitting effects, others observe large CSI signals in non-spin-splitting RuO₂, which are instead explained by relativistic inverse spin Hall effects. In this work, based on first-principles calculations, we reveal that these controversial experimental results originate from a phase-dependent coexistence and relative dominan…

item.title: 通过稀土金属间化合物磁性薄膜的 Josephson 结的反常相移与超导二极管效应
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 G. A. Bobkov, I. A. Shvets, I. V. Bobkova, A. M. Bobkov, S. V. Eremeev, E. V. Chulkov
作者理论研究以超薄 GdIr₂Si₂ 镧系金属间化合物薄膜为势垒的 S/F/S Josephson 结。结合 DFT 计算的电子结构与磁性，构建与之高度一致的紧束缚有效哈密顿量，得到展现反常相移 φ₀≠0,π 及 Josephson 二极管效应的电流-相位关系。该 φ₀ 结允许零场二极管效率与极性的可控调节，并提供超导相位与磁化间的耦合，可用于超导存储与逻辑电路。

item.title: 半金属铁磁体/d波超导体异质结的反常自旋泵浦行为
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hadi H. Hassan, Santiago J. Carreira, M. Cabero, F. Martinet, Alexander Buzdin, Jacobo Santamaria, Javier E. Villegas
作者在外延YBa2Cu3O7-d/La0.7Sr0.3MnO3异质结中研究d波超导体与半金属铁磁体配对下的自旋泵浦，比较c轴和(103)取向两种晶向。通过铁磁共振测得Gilbert阻尼α(T)：(103)样品中α在Tc以下先减小后增大，可由超导能隙打开及节点准粒子主导的自旋输运解释；而c轴样品则在Tc以下显著增强并在0.65–0.7Tc处出现峰值，提示与d波序参量相关的反常自旋耗散机制。

item.title: 扩展 s 波交替磁体
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Matteo Dürrnagel, Lennart Klebl, Tobias Müller, Ronny Thomale, Michael Klett
作者提出扩展 s 波交替磁体（sAM）这一新类磁性态：体系完全打开能隙、自旋补偿，但具有自旋极化能带。sAM 由超出标准晶体自旋群分类的能谷交换对称性（即动量空间平移）所形成。基于双能谷有效模型，作者证明 sAM 表现出各向同性自旋劈裂、可在异质结构中实现自旋选择输运，并诱导子代配对密度波序，最后给出在量子磁体中识别 sAM 的指导原则。

item.title: 应用于铁电向列液晶反铁电序的Landau理论
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Manisha Badu, Arjun Ghimire, Milon, Priyanka Kumari, Hari Krishna Bisoyi, Oleg D. Lavrentovich, James Gleeson, Antal Jakli, Samuel Sprunt
对原型单组分DIO和商用混合物FNLC919两种铁电向列液晶，实验研究反铁电序相关极化与密度调制随温度的演化。通过小角X射线衍射从弱近晶型密度波（q_S=2q_A）确定调制波数q_A，并用描述固体铁电中顺/反铁/铁电相变序列的Landau理论分析。结果显示DIO反铁电相中极化调制接近简谐正弦，而FNLC919在反铁电-铁电转变附近发展出强烈的孤子型轮廓及急剧下降的波数。

item.title: Na₅Yb(MoO₄)₄的磁性与自旋动力学：弱相互作用稀土拉伸金刚石格点
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 N. Rajeesh Kumar, J. Khatua, Changhyun Koo, Izumi Umegaki, C. -E. Yin, C. -W. Wang, A. M. Strydom, H. -T. Jeng, Kwang-Yong Choi, R. Sankar, W. -T. Chen
Na₅Yb(MoO₄)₄属于拉伸金刚石磁格家族，Yb离子间距达6.33 Å。中子衍射、磁化率与比热测量显示其在60 mK以上无长程磁序，低温由Jeff=1/2 Kramers二重态主导。DFT+U计算表明Yb间交换作用因O–Mo–O超超交换路径而可忽略，比热数据显示存在与偶极关联相关的有能隙自旋激发特征。

item.title: 范德瓦尔斯铁磁体与超导谐振腔耦合中的磁振子极化激元
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Alvaro Bermejillo-Seco, Luuk J. van der Goot, Matteo Arfini, Yaroslav M. Blanter, Gary A. Steele, Herre S. J. van der Zant
作者在厚度低至30 nm的Cr₂Ge₂Te₆剥离薄片中观察到与低阻抗超导谐振腔的磁振子-光子杂化。六个器件均出现可重复的回避交叉，耦合强度随厚度的平方根变化，外推显示单层极限下杂化仍可实现，为二维磁体与超导电路集成提供了途径。

item.title: Cs₁₋δV₂Te₂O 中隐藏交替磁性的观测
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Guowei Yang, Ruihan Chen, Changchao Liu, Jing Li, Ze Pan, Teng Hua, Pengyue Xiong, Liwei Deng, Naifu Zheng, Yu Tang, Hao Zheng, Weifan Zhu, Yifu Xu, Xinying Zheng, Xin Ma, Xiaoping Wang, Shengtao Cui, Zhe Sun, Zhengtai Liu, Mao Ye, Chao Cao, Ming Shi, Lunhui Hu, Qihang Liu, Shan Qiao, Guanghan Cao, Yu Song, Yang Liu
作者结合中子衍射、ARPES、自旋分辨 ARPES 和 DFT，发现 Cs₁₋δV₂Te₂O 实现了空间调制形式的交替磁性——隐藏交替磁性。其源于体系的 G 型反铁磁序与二维电子态，使空间交替的交替磁层得以发展，自旋分辨 ARPES 直接验证了各层的局域自旋极化。该发现拓展了非常规磁性的范畴，为探索交替磁序实空间调制衍生现象开辟了新方向。

item.title: 强相互作用量子霍尔MoSe₂单层中反铁磁关联的光学特征
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jiho Sung, Pavel A. Volkov, Ilya Esterlis, Jue Wang, Luke N. Holtzmann, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Katayun Barmak, James Hone, Mikhail D. Lukin, Philip Kim, Hongkun Park
利用磁光谱研究MoSe₂单层在垂直磁场下形成的朗道能级，揭示出强关联电子液体中倾向于非极化态的非常规量子霍尔关联。激子-极化子能量随磁场和电子密度的振荡显示了关联电子液体中朗道能级的出现及不同谷的能级交叉；在低朗道填充因子下，交叉系统性变宽，表明完全极化所需的Zeeman能增加，揭示出反铁磁型关联。

item.title: 二维反铁磁量子临界点处的交错自旋磁化率
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Y. Itoh
作者基于自洽重整化理论，研究了具有零点量子涨落、刚好处于量子临界点(y₀=0)的二维反铁磁自旋涨落，计算了有限温度下交错自旋磁化率χ(Q)随模-模耦合常数y₁的变化。结果表明y₁=0.1是分界：当y₁<0.1时χ(Q)呈Curie行为，y₁>0.1时呈Curie-Weiss型或幂律型温度依赖。

item.title: kagome铁磁体Fe₃Sn₂锥形态磁化率的普适性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Lilian Prodan, Donald M. Evans, Lukas Puntigam, 1 István Kézsmárki, Vladimir Tsurkan
作者报道了kagome铁磁体Fe₃Sn₂自旋重取向(SR)过渡中锥形相微分磁化率(DMS)的普适行为。在SR温区内，DMS等温线汇聚于极窄的等吸光点，通过等吸光不变量分析表明等温线可坍缩为单一温度无关曲线，揭示磁化率的T²修正。磁力显微术显示约0.6 T等吸光场附近自旋织构从条纹畴演化为孤立泡状畴，提示磁晶各向异性、偶极相互作用与外场竞争驱动了等吸光点附近复杂磁织构的普适形成机制。

item.title: Spintronic Bayesian Hardware Driven by Stochastic Magnetic Domain Wall Dynamics
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Science: Table of Contents
👤 Tianyi Wang, Bingqian Dai, Shijie Xu, Kin Wong, Yaochen Li, Yang Cheng, Qingyuan Shu, Haoran He, Puyang Huang, Hanshen Huang, Xixiang Zhang, Kang L. Wang
Advanced Science, Volume 13, Issue 27, 13 May 2026.

item.title: 具有大压电效应的无金属分子铁电体及其多孔复合材料实现超高输出功率密度
item.description:
📚 Wiley: Advanced Science: Table of Contents
👤 Nan Chen, Wenjuan Wei, Le Ye, Lingyu Wan, Yen Wei
该工作报道了一种无金属分子铁电材料，兼具显著压电效应；将其与多孔基质复合后获得了优异的输出功率密度，详情需查阅原文。

item.title: Tangent-Plane Evidential Uncertainty in Active Learning for Magnetic Interatomic Potentials
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yang Cheng, Hongyu Yu, Hongjun Xiang
arXiv:2605.12353v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Magnetic interatomic potentials need to account for coupled lattice and spin degrees of freedom, yet constructing reliable training sets remains costly because noncollinear first-principles labels are expensive. Active learning can mitigate this cost, provided that the uncertainty estimate is physically meaningful for the magnetic-response targets that drive spin reorientation. Here we extend the e²IP evidential framework to magnetic machine-learning interatomic potentials by formulating the projected spin-force likelihood and the corresponding epistemic uncertainty in the tangent plane orthogonal to the local spin direction. This construction prevents the uncertainty model from allocating probability mass to a radial spin component that is…

item.title: Probabilistic Computers for Neural Quantum States
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Shuvro Chowdhury, Jasper Pieterse, Navid Anjum Aadit, Shaila Niazi, Johan H. Mentink, Kerem Y. Camsari
arXiv:2512.24558v2 Announce Type: replace-cross Abstract: Neural quantum states efficiently represent many-body wavefunctions with neural networks, but the cost of Monte Carlo sampling limits their scaling to large system sizes. Here we address this challenge by combining sparse Boltzmann machine architectures with probabilistic computing hardware. We implement a probabilistic computer on field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) and use it as a fast sampler for energy-based neural quantum states. For the two-dimensional transverse-field Ising model at criticality, we obtain accurate ground-state energies for lattices up to 80×80 (6400 spins) using a custom multi-FPGA cluster. Furthermore, we introduce a dual-sampling algorithm to train deep Boltzmann machines, replacing intractable marginal…

item.title: Low-energy DNA bubble dynamics via the quantum Coulomb potential
item.description:
📚 The Journal of Chemical Physics Current Issue
We developed a low-energy model that can be used at any time to describe the dynamics of deoxyribonucleic acid bubbles at temperatures below the melting point. The Schrödinger equation associated with this problem is solved in imaginary time with a quantum Coulomb potential, and we obtain an approximate expression for its more general physical solution as a linear combination of the states whose energies are close to the lower bound energy. We can then determine the probability density, the first-passage time density, and the correlation functions in terms of Bessel functions. Our findings are consistent with results obtained directly from the Fokker–Planck equation and offer a closed expression for the mentioned functions in contrast to the asymptotic results for large times previously r…

item.title: A method for predicting thermal error in machine tool spindles based on multi-feature GRU network
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Jianfeng Mao, Yongjian Li, Qinghui Xue, Zhousheng Zheng, Bin Sun, Peng Sun 等7位作者
Thermal deformation of machine tool spindles is a major source of machining errors. Effective thermal error compensation remains challenging due to the difficulty in selecting representative temperature measurement points and developing prediction models capable of handling temperature fluctuations and thermal hysteresis. This study proposes an integrated thermal error prediction method for CNC machine tool spindles based on virtual temperature sensors and a multi-feature gated recurrent unit (GRU)-based model. The thermal behavior of the spindle system is analyzed using finite element simulation, and surface mesh nodes of the spindle housing are regarded as virtual temperature sensors. Based on the simulated temperature data, k-means clustering combined with grey relational analysis is a…

item.title: Tailoring the material properties, nanostructure and grain alignment of Alnico magnets through micromagnetic simulations
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Anda Elena Stanciu, Johann Fischbacher, Markus Gusenbauer, Alexander Kovacs, Harald Oezelt, Joachim Seland Graff, Patricia Carvalho, Anette Eleonora Gunnæs, Matej Zaplotnik, Espen Sagvolden, Spyros Diplas, Thomas Schrefl
arXiv:2605.11982v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Alnico magnets have gained renewed interest in the search for rare-earth free permanent magnets due to their high thermal stability and magnetisation. However, the limited coercivity of these shape-anisotropy-based alloys constrains their performance. Starting from a reference Alnico sample, we realised a finite elements micromagnetic study of exchange-decoupled rods by varying their dimensions and interrod spacing across those observed experimentally. We computed the hysteresis properties by progressing from micromagnetic simulations of a small number of rods within the magnetostatic field of their neighbours to large systems treated statistically based on the distribution of orientations of the grains. We compared the coercivity of an isol…

item.title: Phase-slip residual-order spin state in FeSe
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zhixin Liu, Jiyu Fan, Lei Zhang, Ma Chunlan, Yanda Ji, Zhongqin Yang, Yan Zhu
arXiv:2605.12293v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Clarifying the magnetic ground state is essential for analysing unconventional superconductivity, because microscopic magnetic order provides one of the basic starting assumptions for spin-fluctuation pairing theories. FeSe exhibits pronounced stripe- and Neel-type spin fluctuations yet lacks static long-range order, posing a long-standing puzzle. By combining PBE and r2SCAN mixed exchange-correlation calculations with spectrally weighted simulations of the static spin structure factor S(q), we show that FeSe is not governed by a single magnetic configuration but by a nearly degenerate manifold of phase-slip defects embedded in a stripe background. We term this state a residual-order spin state (ROSS): a spin state that retains local stripe-…

item.title: Scalable Measurement-Based Quantum Simulation Patterns for Benchmarking
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 V. W. Scarola
arXiv:2605.12502v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Measurement-based quantum computing uses measurement patterns on predefined quantum resource states to execute quantum logic. Quantum simulation offers an important use case on near-term devices. However, pattern optimization depends on the multivariable interplay between hardware and software constraints and is therefore use-dependent and highly non-trivial. Optimization of large-scale patterns under realistic assumptions remains a barrier. We announce the release of the quantum measurement pattern library QPatLib, a dataset that, in v1.0, presents patterns for use in measurement-based quantum simulation. We present the workflow for generating patterns that execute Pauli-string unitaries needed for many quantum algorithms. We provide benc…

item.title: Understanding oxide-thickness-dependent variability in dense Si-MOS quantum dot arrays
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Arne Loenders, Jacques Van Damme, Clement Godfrin, Paola Favia, Jacopo Franco, Thomas Van Caekenberghe, Bart Raes, Gulzat Jaliel, Sylvain Baudot, Luis Francisco Pinotti, Alexander Grill, George Simion, Kristof Moors, Vukan Levajac, Sofie Beyne, Sugandha Sharma, Stefan Kubicek, Yosuke Shimura, Roger Loo, Massimo Mongillo, Danny Wan, Kristiaan De Greve
arXiv:2605.12143v2 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Achieving uniform and scalable control of semiconductor spin qubits remains a key challenge for large scale quantum computing. In this work, we investigate how gate oxide thickness influences uniformity in dense two dimensional silicon quantum dot arrays. Using a 7 x 7 array fabricated in a 300 mm CMOS-process patterned by EUV lithography, we statistically characterize 392 quantum dots across four different oxide thicknesses. The threshold voltages, capacitances, lever arms, and charging energies are extracted using parallel row based measurements and we identify an optimal SiO2 thickness of 17 nm that minimizes threshold voltage variability below 63 mV standard deviation. Our observations illustrate how multiple sources of disorder can in…

item.title: 旋转旋量偶极量子液滴中的Barnett效应
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Donghao Yan, Shaoxiong Li, Hiroki Saito
作者提出释放自旋自由度以稳定偶极玻色-爱因斯坦凝聚自束缚液滴中的涡旋态。当液滴嵌入涡旋时，类Barnett效应使轨道角动量转移为自旋角动量，沿轴向产生自发磁化；外加磁场后整个原子云不变形地整体旋转，可视为宏观Larmor进动。手性相反的液滴对可因自发磁化间的吸引相互作用形成稳定束缚态。

item.title: 同构蜂窝磁体CrCl₃与α-RuCl₃的结构可逆性与磁关联对比
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zachary Morgan (Neutron Scattering Division, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Oak Ridge, Tennessee, USA), Iris Ye (Next Generation Pathway to Computing Program Participant), Jiasen Guo (Neutron Scattering Division, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Oak Ridge, Tennessee, USA), Michael A McGuire (Materials Science and Technology Division, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Oak Ridge, Tennessee, USA), Jiaqiang Yan (Materials Science and Technology Division, Oak Ridge National Laboratory, Oak Ridge, Tennessee, USA)
通过中子单晶衍射比较研究两种层状卤化物CrCl₃与α-RuCl₃，二者均在C2/m与R-3之间发生一阶结构相变。c轴均呈阶跃变化，但α-RuCl₃面内晶格突变并伴随热循环下的晶体退化，而CrCl₃面内演化平滑且结构稳健。磁性方面，CrCl₃在T_N=14 K形成A型反铁磁并具有延展至约40 K的弥散磁散射，而α-RuCl₃在T_N=7.6 K以上无可观测弥散磁散射，暗示层间耦合与电子结构的相互作用导致两者差异。

item.title: 具有自发旋转对称破缺与非平凡零模的非点群超导体的发现
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Hui Guo, Zhixuan Li, Senhao Lv, Tianqi Gao, Zihao Huang, Kuanrong Hao, Lizhi Zhang, Ke Zhu, Siyu Li, Xianghe Han, Xiao Lin, Shengshan Qin, Wu Zhou, Haitao Yang, Hui Chen, Hong-Jun Gao
鉴定非点群（nonsymmorphic）化合物PtPb₄为承载自发旋转对称破缺超导态及非平凡零能模的稳健平台。PtPb₄以受挫Shastry-Sutherland格子结晶并具非平凡能带拓扑，面内/面外电阻测量显示超导态与上临界场均具显著二重各向异性；扫描隧道显微/谱学观察到二重对称的磁通涡旋及无空间劈裂的稳健零能涡旋束缚态，为对称破缺超导态及拓扑超导提供直接实空间证据。

item.title: 共线磁系统的等变空间群及哈密顿量
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chaoxi Cui, Zhi-Ming Yu, Yilin Han, Run-Wu Zhang, Shengyuan A. Yang, Yugui Yao
针对共线磁性材料中以磁序方向n为调控参数的需求，作者基于等变空间群建立构造显含n依赖的有效哈密顿量(EMH)的对称性框架。EMH定义于扩展的k-n空间，呈现非常规对称作用与拓扑特征。以一维铁磁链和三维反铁磁体为例，展示磁动力学驱动的拓扑泵浦，包括偶数整数电荷泵浦和二阶Chern数诱导的表面反常Hall电导量子化泵浦；该框架还可结合第一性原理计算并推广至非共线磁系统。

item.title: 莫尔分数Chern绝缘体中的激子-roton模
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Xiaoyang Shen, Zijian Zhou, Ruiping Guo, Renqi Wang, Wenhui Duan, Chong Wang, Yong Xu
作者证明在莫尔分数Chern绝缘体(FCI)中，磁roton与莫尔带间激发的杂化在长波极限下产生连续FQH体系中所没有的激子-roton模。通过对扭转MoTe₂的精确对角化和变分Bethe-Salpeter方程，表明该杂化由量子几何调控，产生同时具有激子光学响应和FCI roton极小特征的模式。该模式能量低于带间跃迁并获得光学活性，呈双峰光谱特征，使光学谱学成为莫尔FCI中集体激发的直接探针。

item.title: 几何设计赋能超导纳米线单光子探测器的磁韧性
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Marinus C. van der Maas, Lin Jin, Ilhan Tunç, Raymond Vermeulen, Henri Ervasti, Ravi Gopie, Jan Riegelmeyer, Marco Colangelo, Ryoichi Ishihara, Carlos Errando-Herranz
磁场显著降低超导纳米线单光子探测器（SNSPD）的探测效率，限制其在磁性光子学中的应用。系统表征了NbTiN SNSPD在磁场下的性能，发现内禀探测效率在低偏置电流下增强、高电流下抑制，导致饱和平台消失；该退化幅度强烈依赖于纳米线宽度。通过宽度优化，实现了在应用相关磁场下对宽光子能量范围均具有饱和IDE的SNSPD，为探测器集成的自旋-光学及原子量子处理器等应用奠定基础。

item.title: 磁场可调的回旋双曲极化激元
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Zijian Zhou, Ran Jing, Heng Wang, Lukas Wehmeier, Mengkun Liu, Bing Cheng
提出一类源于磁场诱导回旋运动的新型双曲极化激元。当垂直磁场施加于高迁移率半金属时，回旋响应使面内介电函数在回旋共振频率以下由金属型转为绝缘型，而面外保持金属型，从而形成支持场可调双曲极化激元的各向异性介电环境。作者建立了包含与其他集体激发耦合的理论框架，并表明这些模式可通过太赫兹近场纳米显微在实空间直接成像，确立了磁可编程纳米光子学的新平台。

item.title: 螺位错GaAs量子线的光学响应：温度与压力效应
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Vinod Kumar, Shweta Kumari, Surender Pratap
本文研究螺位错对抛物型GaAs圆柱量子线光学响应的影响，并考虑温度、静水压力与轴向磁场的耦合作用。采用扭转修正度规及压力温度依赖的有效质量与介电常数，利用Whittaker函数得到Schrödinger方程的精确解，进而计算线性与三阶非线性吸收系数及折射率变化。结果表明螺位错破坏了角动量±m态的对称性：m=0→+1跃迁出现明显红移与共振幅度抑制，而m=0→−1跃迁则呈现蓝移。

item.title: 将 Wigner 晶体量子熔化为霍尔液体
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Aidan P. Reddy, Liang Fu
近期实验反直觉地显示磁场可在 Wigner 晶体中诱导量子霍尔效应。作者通过变分蒙特卡罗方法证明，磁场能够将零场 Wigner 晶体熔化为整数量子霍尔液体。这种熔化源自液体基态能量在整数填充因子处因不可压缩性而出现向下尖点的量子振荡，并确定了发生该量子熔化转变的密度范围。

item.title: 量子隧穿、全局相位与经典作用重构的极限
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Chong Qi, Mário B. Amaro
近期有人提出可用经典作用分支与对应经典密度的离散叠加精确重构Schrödinger波函数。本文检验该构造在有限势垒量子隧穿与量子相位现象中的适用性。研究表明，当Hamilton-Jacobi方程存在全局实分支时构造形式上自洽，但在不存在实经典作用的经典禁区会失效，需引入非零量子势或复值作用。以α衰变和核聚变中的矩形和库仑势垒、Berry相、磁通量子化、Josephson隧穿及dc SQUID干涉为例，说明全局相位约束无法由局部实经典轨迹给出。

item.title: 中子星外核超流-超导涡旋态的平均场理论
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Dmitry Kobyakov
作者将现有的平均场理论扩展至质子与中子配对临界温度Tcₚ≠Tcₙ的情形，假定两者均由自旋0的Cooper对形成。基于有效手性场论确定配对能隙Δα₀的压力依赖，并通过弱耦合近似Tcα≈0.57Δα₀联系临界温度。结果显示超流牵连效应增大涡旋核尺寸并减小有效磁穿透深度，外核区中子涡旋核大于磁穿透深度，挑战了通常的近似处理。

item.title: Machida-Shibata 晶格中的时间反演对称拓扑超导
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ioannis Ioannidis, Ching-Kai Chiu, Thore Posske
作者基于近期在超导体上的吸附原子笼中实现的自旋简并 Andreev 束缚态（Machida-Shibata 态），研究多态杂化形成的时间反演对称能带及其在弱在位电子排斥下的拓扑性质。低能理论显示，单态与三重态超导配对的竞争驱动系统进入 DIII 类拓扑非平凡相：一维链端出现 Majorana 零模 Kramers 对，二维晶格出现螺旋 Majorana 边缘模。研究还发现布里渊区中存在大范围配对消失区域，电子排斥可将其打开，为非磁性吸附原子体系中调控拓扑超导提供新途径。

item.title: 竞争性密度波与超导残余序的无偏大N方法
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Grgur Palle, Rafael M. Fernandes
多分量主序参量可支持多个由二次复合序参量描述的残余（vestigial）通道，但标准大N方法在分解复合序参量时存在内在歧义，导致残余不稳定性可被人为放大或消除。作者证明这种歧义源于Fierz恒等式等冗余关系，反映不同残余通道间的相互干涉。为此提出一种尊重冗余关系的无偏大N方案，可统一处理iron pnictides、铜氧化物、扭转双层石墨烯等系统中的残余相竞争。

item.title: Measuring Accuracy and Energy-to-Solution of Quantum Fine-Tuning of Foundational AI Models
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Oliver Knitter, Sang Hyub Kim, Maximilian Wurzer, Jonathan Mei, Claudio Girotto, Karen Horovitz, Chi Chen, Masako Yamada, Frederik F. Flöther, Martin Roetteler
arXiv:2605.02798v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: We present an experimental study of energy-to-solution (ETS) of hybrid quantum-classical applications, enabled by direct instrumentation of power consumption of a Forte Enterprise trapped-ion quantum processor. We apply this methodology to a hybrid quantum-classical pipeline for quantum fine-tuning of foundational AI models, and validate the approach end-to-end on quantum hardware. Despite noise and limited qubit counts, the resulting models achieve accuracy competitive with and exceeding classical baselines such as logistic regression and support vector classifiers. Our results show that QPU energy consumption scales approximately linearly with qubit number for shallow circuits, while classical simulation exhibits exponential scaling, ind…

item.title: A Reproducible Workflow for Extracting Quantum Hamiltonians from Surface-Adsorbate Models
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Manisha Malhotra, Fabio Rinaldi, Neil Ramarapu
Bridging the gap between realistic surface catalysis models from density functional theory (DFT) and the input requirements of quantum algorithms is a non-trivial task. Surface DFT calculations typically model extended slabs and yield total energies or reaction energetics, whereas quantum algorithms (such as variational quantum eigensolvers) require a many-body Hamiltonian (e.g. a list of fermionic or qubit operators) as input. We present an open-source, reproducible software workflow that takes an optimized DFT slab model of a catalyst surface and produces a few-body Hamiltonian suitable for quantum simulation. Starting from a DFT-optimized slab geometry, the workflow extracts a localized cluster around the active site, identifies an appropriate set of active orbitals, and constructs the…

item.title: Secure Multi-Party Computation Based on Blind Quantum Computing
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Yongli Wang, Tianci Cao, Yixin Wang, Rui Zhang, Yinghui Yang, Yongli Tang
Blind Quantum Computing (BQC) is a protocol proposed in recent years to tackle the practical need where users want to perform quantum computing tasks but lack the necessary capabilities, and thus delegate the work to quantum computing platforms while keeping their data secure throughout the process. Besides, its characteristics provide new ways for secure multi-party computation, helping multiple untrusted users to compute together securely. Most existing secure multi-party computation protocol based on blind quantum computing rely on DT(G) states to perform joint computation. However, this can lead to security risks because one client has too much authority, and these schemes also lack generality. In this paper, a secure multi-party computation protocol is proposed based on brickwork sta…

item.title: Topological superconducting phase in a non-Hermitian Kitaev chain with staggered pairing imbalance
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Xiao-Jue Zhang, Rong Lü, and Qi-Bo Zeng
Author(s): Xiao-Jue Zhang, Rong Lü, and Qi-Bo Zeng We introduce a one-dimensional non-Hermitian Kitaev chain with staggered imbalance in the p-wave superconducting pairing. By tuning the chemical potential and the pairing imbalance, we find that the eigenenergy spectrum undergoes real-to-complex transitions, and the spectral gap can change from a … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195408] Published Wed May 13, 2026

item.title: Topological superconductivity and superconducting diode effect mediated via unconventional magnet and Ising spin-orbit coupling
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Amartya Pal, Debashish Mondal, Tanay Nag, and Arijit Saha
Author(s): Amartya Pal, Debashish Mondal, Tanay Nag, and Arijit Saha We propose a theoretical framework in which a one-dimensional (1D) tight-binding model incorporating unconventional magnetic order together with Rashba and Ising spin-orbit couplings are considered to realize two key phenomena in condensed matter systems: topological superconductivity and the superc… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195409] Published Wed May 13, 2026

item.title: PAEMS: Precise and Adaptive Error Model for Superconducting Quantum Processors
item.description:
📚 Preprints
👤 Songhuan He, Yifei Cui, Cheng Wang
Superconducting quantum processor units (QPUs) are incapable of producing massive datasets for quantum error correction (QEC) because of hardware limitations. Thus, QEC decoders heavily depend on synthetic data from qubit error models. Classic depolarizing error models with polynomial complexity present limited accuracy. Coherent density matrix methods suffer from exponential complexity ∝ O(4 n) where n represents the number of qubits. This paper introduces PAEMS: a precise and adaptive qubit error model. Its qubit-wise separation framework, incorporating leakage propagation, captures error evolvements crossing spatial and temporal domains. Utilizing repetition-code experiment datasets, PAEMS effectively identifies the intrinsic qubit errors through an end-to-end optimization pipeline. Ex…

item.title: Machine Learning for neutron source distributions
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Jose Ignacio Robledo, Norberto Schmidt, Klaus Lieutenant, Jingjing Li, Stefan Kesselheim, Paul Zakalek
arXiv:2605.12165v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In light of the recent advancements in machine learning, we propose a novel approach to neutron source distribution estimation through the utilisation of probabilistic generative models. The estimation is based on a Monte Carlo particle list, which is only required during the training stage of the machine learning model. Once the source distribution has been learned, the model is independent of the original particle list, allowing for further sampling in an efficient, rapid, and memory-costless manner. The performance of various generative models is evaluated, including a variational autoencoder, a normalizing flow, a generative adversarial network, and a denoising diffusion model. These approaches are then compared to existing source distri…

item.title: Global Tensor Network Renormalization for 2D Quantum systems: A new window to probe universal data from thermal transitions
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Atsushi Ueda, Sander De Meyer, Adwait Naravane, Victor Vanthilt, Frank Verstraete
arXiv:2508.05406v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We propose a new tensor network renormalization group (TNR) scheme based on global optimization and introduce a new method for constructing the finite-temperature density matrix of two-dimensional quantum systems. Combining these two into a new algorithm called thermal tensor network renormalization (TTNR), we obtain highly accurate conformal field theory (CFT) data at thermal transition points. This provides a new and efficient route for numerically identifying phase transitions, offering an alternative to the conventional analysis via critical exponents.

item.title: Size Extensive Auxiliary-Field Quantum Monte Carlo with Perturbative Coupled Cluster Trial Wavefunction
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Yichi Zhang, Ankit Mahajan, Yann Damour, Sandeep Sharma
arXiv:2605.11257v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: In this work, we develop a size extensive Auxiliary-Field Quantum Monte Carlo (AFQMC) approach that scales as O(N⁵⁾ for local energy evaluation by treating the Coupled Cluster Singles and Doubles (CCSD) trial wavefunctions perturbatively. Comprehensive numerical examinations, spanning from main-group molecules to 3d transition metal complexes, demonstrate that this perturbative treatment introduces negligible bias. For small systems, our method achieves an accuracy and level of noise comparable to AFQMC with configuration interaction singles and doubles (CISD) trial wavefunctions while outperforming CCSD(T). This size extensivity offers a decisive advantage for large systems, as suggested by the ground state energies of non-interacting monom…

item.title: Quantum criticality and nonequilibrium dynamics on a Lieb lattice of Rydberg atoms
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Mark R. Hirsbrunner, Milan Kornjača, Rhine Samajdar, Siva Darbha, Majd Hamdan, Jan Balewski, Ermal Rrapaj, Sheng-Tao Wang, Daan Camps, Fangli Liu, Pedro L. S. Lopes, Katherine Klymko
arXiv:2508.05737v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: Neutral-atom quantum simulators offer a promising approach to the exploration of strongly interacting many-body systems, with applications spanning condensed matter, statistical mechanics, and high-energy physics. Through a combination of quantum experiments, numerical calculations, and analytical methods, we demonstrate a rich set of phenomena accessible on such quantum simulators by studying an array of Rydberg atoms placed on the Lieb lattice. First, we map out the ground states and phase diagram of the system, identifying a range of density-wave-ordered phases -- including a collinear phase stabilized purely by quantum fluctuations -- and find good agreement between theory and experiment. Allowing for local control of the detuning fi…

item.title: CERTIFY-ED：量子多体系统精确对角化的多层验证框架
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Sarang Vehale, Ritu Goel
作者指出现有精确对角化(ED)结果普遍缺乏可机器校验的数值正确性证据，并提出CERTIFY-ED框架，可与QuSpin、XDiag、ALPS等现有ED包配合使用。框架包括：运行三条独立LAPACK路径并报告差异的多oracle本征求解器、覆盖代数不变量/解析极限/守恒律/动力学一致性/有限尺寸标度等13个独立验证层，以及SHA-256防篡改证书，便于下游验证。

item.title: 时变量子谐振子中的压缩与绝热性破缺
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Mattia Orlandini, Beatrice Donelli, Lorenzo Buffoni, Stefano Gherardini
综述基于Lewis-Riesenfeld不变量法、Bogoliubov变换和Ermakov-Pinney方程，统一处理含时频率的量子谐振子。阐述这些方法如何自然连接到描述激发产生的压缩形式，以及一般频率协议下绝热性的破缺，详细讨论了突然淬火与平滑斜坡的精确解。该框架可应用于热力学、凝聚态及量子控制理论中的非平衡动力学。

item.title: Separation of the Kibble-Zurek mechanism from quantum criticality
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. Research
👤 R. Jafari and Alireza Akbari
Author(s): R. Jafari and Alireza Akbari The emergence of Kibble-Zurek scaling is shown to be controlled by the quasiparticle modes participating in the dynamics rather than by quantum criticality alone. As a result, quenches across critical points may remain effectively adiabatic, while noncritical quenches can still exhibit conventional Kibble-Zurek scaling. [Phys. Rev. Research 8, L022027] Published Wed May 13, 2026

item.title: A multi-fidelity Bayesian neural operator for mechanics of spinodal metamaterial
item.description:
📚 npj Computational Materials
👤 Pu You, Hongshun Chen, Bahador Bahmani, Horacio D. Espinosa
npj Computational Materials, Published online: 13 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41524-026-02112-y A multi-fidelity Bayesian neural operator for mechanics of spinodal metamaterial

item.title: Integrated neural wave-function solver for spinful Fermi systems
item.description:
📚 Phys. Rev. B
👤 Alexander Avdoshkin, Max Geier, and Liang Fu
Author(s): Alexander Avdoshkin, Max Geier, and Liang Fu We present an approach to solving the ground state of Fermi systems that contain spin or other discrete degrees of freedom in addition to continuous coordinates. The approach combines a Markov chain Monte Carlo sampling for energy estimation that we adapted to cover the extended configuration space … [Phys. Rev. B 113, 195108] Published Wed May 13, 2026

item.title: Quantum Advantage in Storage and Retrieval of Isometry Channels
item.description:
📚 Editors' Suggestions
👤 Satoshi Yoshida, Jisho Miyazaki, and Mio Murao
Author(s): Satoshi Yoshida, Jisho Miyazaki, and Mio Murao For isometry channels, quantum protocols based on port-based teleportation outperform classical estimate-and-prepare strategies in terms of both query complexity and program cost, achieving a provable asymptotic advantage. [Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 190601] Published Wed May 13, 2026

item.title: 极简硬-软双网络中的缺陷屏蔽与载荷传递
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Fucheng Tian, Feixue Lu, Katsuhiko Sato, Liangbin Li, Bin Li, Jian Ping Gong
构造由两个耦合的无序线弹性网络组成的极简三维双网络模型，复现屈服、颈缩、应变硬化和脆-韧转变等典型非线性力学行为。硬软网络之间的力学反差驱动网络间载荷传递，从而屏蔽缺陷并抑制硬网络中的应力集中。定义应力集中因子Kₛc后发现硬网络失效应变普遍按1/Kₛc标度，将微观缺陷屏蔽与宏观屈服直接联系；完全屏蔽导致从局部颈缩向离域损伤的转变。

item.title: 多孔介质中奥斯特瓦尔德熟化的时间依赖孔隙网络模型
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Ademola Isaac Adebimpe, Sajjad Foroughi, Branko Bijeljic, Martin J. Blunt
建立了耦合水相瞬态传质、毛细压力非均质性及真实孔喉几何的时间依赖孔隙网络模型，刻画多孔介质中气体团簇在奥斯特瓦尔德熟化过程中的动态演化。以Bentheimer砂岩为例，追踪了吸排水事件统计、毛细压力平衡、团簇体积分布及空间饱和度剖面的48小时演变，揭示了体积加权平均毛细压力恒定而数量加权压力快速下降、团簇向更大尺寸集中的特征，以及大孔隙中持续气体捕获现象。

item.title: Bridging scales in porous media: cDFT-informed pore network modelling for fluid transport with nanoconfined phase behavior
item.description:
📚 arXiv
👤 Irina Nesterova, Rustem Sirazov, Aleksey Khlyupin
arXiv:2512.21990v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The simulation of fluid flow in real, multiscale porous media remains challenging due to the complexity of nanoscale phenomena and the difficulty of developing upscaling methodologies. In this study, we introduce a multiscale filtration framework based on quasi-static Pore Network Modelling, incorporating the effects of pore blockage resulting from capillary condensation of fluid in the nanoporous space. To accurately predict capillary condensation in nanoconfinement, we apply classical Density Functional Theory calculations considering capillary hysteresis. The pores blocked by condensate are excluded from the fluid flow, resulting in a decrease in permeability of the porous space. Our findings demonstrate that the resulting permeabilit…
